zh.lifeSummary: 當Sarah Rhodes於1717年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州斯塔福德時，她的父親John Rhodes28歲，母親Anne Paine25歲。她於1737年與Henry Netherton II在美國維吉尼亞州威斯特摩蘭結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1785年在美國維吉尼亞州奧古斯塔享年68歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sarah Rhodes于1717年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州斯塔福德时，她的父亲John Rhodes28岁，母亲Anne Paine25岁。她于1737年与Henry Netherton II在美国弗吉尼亚州威斯特摩蘭结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1785年在美国弗吉尼亚州奧古斯塔享年68岁。

nameMeanings.name: Rhodes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: either a topographic name for someone who lived by ‘(the) woodland clearings’, plural form of Middle English rode (Old English rod, rodu), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named, principally Rhodes in Bury (Lancashire) or possibly from one of the many minor places in Yorkshire similarly named, or Rhodes Minnis (Kent). The Yorkshire name sometimes alternates with the singular form (see Rhode and Rode ). The Rh- spelling was introduced in the 16th and 17th centuries by clerks with a classical education, who associated the name with the Greek island of Rhodes, famous in ancient history and mythology. There is also no connection with modern English road (Old English rād ‘riding’), which was not used to denote a thoroughfare until the 16th century. The surname is particularly common in Yorkshire and Lancashire but occurs with various spellings in smaller numbers widely across England.
variant of Rhode , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Rhodus
meanings: Perhaps an altered form of Rhodes or German Rhodius, a Latinized form of Rode , perhaps indicating the personal as opposed to the topographic name (see Rode 1).

variants.name: Rod
meanings: German: habitational name from any of several places called Rod, or a topographic name for somene who lived in a woodland clearing, from a derivative of Middle High German roden ‘to clear land for cultivation’. Compare Rhodes .
Norwegian (Rød): habitational name from any of numerous farmsteads so named, from Old Norse ruth ‘clearing’.
Czech and Croatian: from a short form of the Slavic compound personal names based on the element rod ‘family, relatives’, e.g. Rodoslav.

variants.name: Roades
meanings: English: variant of   Rhodes  . This form of the surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Rhode
meanings: English: topographic name from Middle English rode (Old English rod, rodu) designating someone who lived by a woodland clearing, or for someone who came from a place so named, such as Road (Somerset), Rhode (Somerset), Road Farm in Colebrook (Devon), Rhode in Halberton (Devon), Roade (Northamptonshire), Rode (Cheshire), and Rodd (Herefordshire).
English: topographic name for someone who lived by a reed bed, from Middle English rode (Old English hrēod). See also Rude and Trude .
German: habitational name from any places called Rhode (Lower Saxony, Rhineland) and Rhoden (Hesse, Saxony-Anhalt, former Silesia).

nameMeanings.name: Sarah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the wife of Abraham and mother of Isaac. According to the Book of Genesis, she was originally called Sarai (possibly meaning ‘contentious’ in Hebrew), but had her name changed by God to the more auspicious Sarah ‘princess’ in token of a greater blessing (Genesis 17:15, ‘And God said unto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, thou shalt not call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall her name be’). This has been one of the most enduringly popular girls' names.

variants.name: Sara
meanings: Variant of   Sarah .  This is the form used in the Greek of the New Testament (Hebrews 11:11).

historicEvents.title: Mount Vernon
historicEvents.description: Mount Vernon Plantation was the home of George Washington. It started off as 2,000 acres and was later expanded to 8,000 acres. The house itself started off as a six room building then got extended to twenty-one rooms.
"Patrick Henry made his ""Give me Liberty or Give me Death"" speech in Richmond Virginia."
Thomas Jefferson's American Declaration of Independence endorsed by Congress. Colonies declare independence.

en.lifeSummary: When Sarah Rhodes was born in 1717, in Stafford, Virginia, British Colonial America, her father, John Rhodes, was 28 and her mother, Anne Paine, was 25. She married Henry Netherton II in 1737, in Westmoreland, Virginia, United States. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1785, in Augusta, Virginia, United States, at the age of 68.

Elle a épousé Henry Netherton II en 1737, à Westmoreland, Virginie, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1785, à Augusta, Virginie, États-Unis, à l’âge de 68 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Sarah Rhodes(이)가 1717년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 스태퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Rhodes(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Anne Paine(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1737년 미국 버지니아 주 웨스트모렐랜드에서 Henry Netherton II(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1785년 미국 버지니아 주 오구스타에서 68세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sarah Rhodes родилась в 1717 г. в местности стаффорд, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика, её отцу по имени John Rhodes было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Anne Paine, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Henry Netherton II в 1737 г. в местности вестмореланд, Вирджиния, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1785 г. в местности аугуста, Вирджиния, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 68 лет.
